algorithms.title: Performing the Pediatric Assessment Triangle
algorithms.description: The hands-off, from-the-doorway assessment performed within the first seconds of any pediatric encounter.
steps: Approach the child without touching; observe from across the room.
Assess Appearance using TICLS: Tone, Interactiveness, Consolability, Look/Gaze, Speech/Cry.
Assess Work of Breathing: listen for abnormal sounds (stridor, grunting, wheeze, muffled voice); look for abnormal positioning, retractions, nasal flaring, head bobbing.
Assess Circulation to Skin: look for pallor, mottling, or cyanosis compared to the child's baseline, in adequate lighting.
Combine all three legs into a pattern to identify the likely physiologic category and urgency.
Proceed immediately to the full hands-on primary assessment (ABCDE), paced according to the PAT's urgency level.

algorithms.title: PAT Pattern-to-Category Recognition
algorithms.description: Translating the combination of abnormal PAT legs into a likely physiologic category.
steps: Abnormal Appearance only → likely CNS/metabolic problem.
Abnormal Appearance + Work of Breathing → likely respiratory problem.
Abnormal Appearance + Circulation to Skin → likely shock/circulatory problem.
Abnormal Appearance + Work of Breathing + Circulation to Skin → cardiopulmonary failure (highest urgency).
All three legs normal → reassuring general impression; proceed to primary assessment at a routine pace.
